1.title: The name Samuel
1.text: The name 'Samuel' recalls that Hannah 'asked him of the Lord.' She dedicated him to God's service in keeping with the vow she made at the temple.

1.question: Why was Hannah in anguish at the start of the book?
options: She was barren and provoked by Peninnah
She had been driven from home

1.question: What did the priest Eli think when he saw Hannah praying silently in the temple?
What vow did Hannah make to the Lord?
options: To dedicate her son to the Lord all the days of his life
To give away all her gold

1.question: What name did Hannah give her son, recalling that she asked him of the Lord?
When did Hannah bring the boy to the temple at Shiloh?
options: On the day he was born

1.question: What did Hannah do with Samuel at the temple of Shiloh?
options: She gave him to the Lord, into Eli's care

2.title: Hannah's song
2.text: Hannah's song, praising the God who 'raiseth up the poor out of the dust,' inspires Mary's Magnificat in the Gospel of Luke.

2.question: What is Hannah's prayer at the start of chapter 2?
options: A song of praise to the Lord

2.question: What theme marks Hannah's song?
options: God brings low the proud and lifts up the humble

2.question: What were Hophni and Phinehas, the sons of Eli, like?
options: Corrupt priests who knew not the Lord

2.question: What was the sin of Eli's sons at the sanctuary?
options: They despised the offerings and abused the people

2.question: What did the Lord do for Hannah after she dedicated Samuel?
How did the boy Samuel grow before the Lord and men?
What did the man of God announce to Eli?
options: That his house would be judged for his sons' sin
That he would live a hundred years
That he would rebuild the temple

2.question: What did God promise to raise up in place of Eli's house?

3.title: The word was rare
3.text: 'The word of the Lord was precious in those days; there was no open vision.' Samuel's call marks the return of the prophetic voice to Israel.

3.question: When the Lord called the boy Samuel at night, what did he think at first?
What did Eli teach Samuel to answer the Lord?
What message did the Lord give Samuel that night?
How did all Israel come to recognize Samuel?
options: As a faithful prophet of the Lord

4.title: I-chabod: 'the glory is departed'
4.text: On hearing the ark was taken, Eli's daughter-in-law gave birth and named the child I-chabod, saying, 'The glory is departed from Israel.'

4.question: What did Israel take into battle, thinking it would defeat the Philistines?
What happened in the battle where the ark was taken?
options: The ark was captured and Eli's sons died

4.question: Who brought Eli the news of the defeat?
options: A man of Benjamin who fled the battle

4.question: What happened to Eli when he heard the ark was taken?
options: He fell off his seat and died

5.title: Dagon, the Philistine idol
5.text: Dagon was the chief god of the Philistines. Before the ark of the Lord his image fell to the ground and broke — showing who the true God is.

5.question: What happened to the idol Dagon before the ark of the Lord?
options: It fell to the ground and broke

5.question: What afflicted the Philistines while they kept the ark?
What did the Philistines finally decide to do with the ark?

6.title: The gold offering
6.text: The Philistines sent with the ark an offering of golden tumors and mice — acknowledging the plague had come from the hand of the God of Israel.

6.question: How did the Philistines return the ark to Israel?
options: On a new cart, with an offering of gold

6.question: What sign would show whether the plague had truly come from the God of Israel?
options: The cows going straight to Beth-shemesh, not turning aside

6.question: What did the men of Beth-shemesh do when they saw the ark arrive?
options: They returned it to the Philistines

6.question: Why were many men of Beth-shemesh struck down?
options: For looking into the ark of the Lord

7.title: Eben-ezer, the stone of help
7.text: Samuel set up a stone and called it Eben-ezer — 'stone of help' — saying, 'Hitherto hath the Lord helped us.'

7.question: What did Samuel ask Israel to do before the victory at Mizpah?
options: Put away the strange gods and serve the Lord only

7.question: How did the Lord defeat the Philistines at Mizpah?
options: He thundered upon them and discomfited them
He sent an angel with a sword

7.question: What name did Samuel give the stone he raised, saying 'Hitherto hath the Lord helped us'?

8.title: Rejecting the King of kings
8.text: When the people asked for a human king, the Lord told Samuel: 'they have not rejected thee, but they have rejected me, that I should not reign over them.'

8.question: What did the elders of Israel ask of Samuel?
options: A king, like the other nations had

8.question: What did Samuel warn about the king they asked for?
options: That he would take their sons, daughters, fields and goods
That he would flee in war

8.question: According to Samuel, what else would the king take from the people?
options: A tenth of the harvests and flocks

8.question: How did the people respond to Samuel's warning?
options: They insisted: 'We will have a king over us'

9.title: Saul, the tallest
9.text: Saul is introduced as a handsome young man, taller than all the people from the shoulders up — and of the small tribe of Benjamin.

9.question: Why had Saul left home, on the occasion he met Samuel?
options: He was seeking his father's lost donkeys

9.question: Whom did Saul and his servant decide to consult about the donkeys?
What was a prophet formerly called in Israel?
What had the Lord revealed to Samuel the day before Saul came?
options: That he would send a man to be anointed captain of Israel

9.question: How did Saul respond to the honor Samuel gave him?
options: He said he was of Benjamin, the smallest tribe

9.question: What did Samuel prepare to do with Saul at dawn?
options: Reveal to him the word of God and anoint him

10.title: 'Is Saul also among the prophets?'
10.text: When Saul prophesied with a band of prophets, his acquaintances were amazed, and the saying arose: 'Is Saul also among the prophets?'

10.question: What did Samuel do to Saul privately?
options: He anointed him with oil as captain of Israel

10.question: What sign happened to Saul, becoming a saying in Israel?
What did Saul tell his uncle about the kingdom?
options: Nothing — he hid the matter of the kingdom
That he would be king tomorrow
That he had refused the crown

10.question: How was Saul publicly chosen king at Mizpah?
Where was Saul found when he was to be presented as king?

11.title: The Spirit moves a king
11.text: When the Spirit of God came upon Saul, his indignation gathered all Israel in a single day to relieve Jabesh-gilead.

11.question: What did Nahash the Ammonite demand of the men of Jabesh-gilead for a covenant?
options: To thrust out the right eye of each one

11.question: How did Saul gather Israel to relieve Jabesh?
options: The Spirit of God came on him and he summoned the people

11.question: What happened after Saul defeated the Ammonites?
options: The kingdom was renewed and confirmed at Gilgal

12.title: Thunder in the harvest
12.text: Samuel called for thunder and rain in the time of wheat harvest — something unusual — as a sign that asking for a king had been a sin before God.

12.question: What did Samuel ask the people to witness about his leadership?
options: That he had never taken from or oppressed anyone
That he had been a great warrior
That he had built the temple
That he had chosen the king alone

12.question: What did Samuel recall to the people in his speech?
options: How God had delivered them since Egypt

12.question: What condition did Samuel set for the king and people to prosper?
options: To fear and obey the Lord

12.question: What sign did God give through Samuel, in the time of wheat harvest?
What did Samuel promise to do, even after the sinful request for a king?
options: Not to cease praying for them and to teach them the good way
To go back to being a judge

13.title: 'A man after his own heart'
13.text: In rejecting Saul's disobedience, Samuel announces that God has sought 'a man after his own heart' to be prince — the first hint of David.

13.question: Who smote the garrison of the Philistines, sparking the conflict?
What was happening while Saul waited for Samuel at Gilgal?
options: The people, afraid, began to scatter

13.question: What error did Saul commit before the battle?
options: He offered the burnt offering without waiting for Samuel

13.question: What did Samuel tell Saul because of his disobedience?
options: That his kingdom would not continue
That he would defeat the Philistines
That he would live a hundred years

13.question: Why did Israel have almost no swords or spears?
options: The Philistines allowed no smiths in Israel

14.title: 'To save by many or by few'
14.text: Before attacking alone with his armourbearer, Jonathan declared: 'there is no restraint to the Lord to save by many or by few.'

14.question: Who went secretly to attack the Philistine garrison, with only his armourbearer?
What principle of faith did Jonathan declare before attacking?
options: It is not hard for the Lord to save by many or by few
That they must wait for Saul

14.question: What sign did Jonathan set to know whether God would deliver them?
options: If the Philistines said 'come up to us'

14.question: What caused panic in the camp of the Philistines?
What rash oath did Saul impose on the army that day?
options: That no one eat until evening, until he was avenged

14.question: What did Jonathan do, not knowing of his father's oath?
options: He ate a little honey and his strength returned

14.question: What sin did the famished people commit at evening?
options: They ate flesh with the blood

14.question: Why did God not answer Saul when he inquired that day?
options: Because of a sin committed in the camp

14.question: On whom did the lot fall, for breaking the oath unknowingly?
What did the people do when Saul would have killed Jonathan over the oath?
options: They rescued him, for he had won the victory

15.title: The rejected king
15.text: After sparing Agag, Saul was finally rejected by God as king; Samuel mourned for him — and the way opened for David's anointing.

15.question: What command did the Lord give Saul through Samuel?
options: To make a covenant with Moab

15.question: How did Saul disobey the Lord?
options: He spared king Agag and the best of the livestock
He did not go to war

15.question: What did the Lord say to Samuel about Saul?
options: That he repented of having made him king
That he would give him a son
That he would take him to Shiloh

15.question: What excuse did Saul give for the spared livestock?
options: That the people kept it to sacrifice to the Lord
That he was too small to stop it
That he had not seen the cattle

15.question: What principle did Samuel declare to Saul?
What did Samuel say would happen to Saul's kingdom?
options: It would be torn away and given to another better than he
It would pass to his son
It would be split in two

15.question: What did Samuel do with Agag, king of the Amalekites?
options: He killed him before the Lord at Gilgal
He took him captive to Shiloh

16.title: God looks on the heart
16.text: When Samuel was impressed by Jesse's eldest son's appearance, the Lord said: 'man looketh on the outward appearance, but the Lord looketh on the heart.'

16.question: Where did God send Samuel to anoint the new king?
options: To the house of Jesse, in Beth-lehem

16.question: What did the Lord teach Samuel about choosing the king?
options: Man looks on appearance, but God looks on the heart
The king must be the tallest
The king must be the eldest
The king must be the richest

16.question: Whom did Samuel anoint, the youngest son of Jesse?
What troubled Saul, leading him to seek a harp player?
How did David enter Saul's service?
options: By playing the harp to relieve him

17.title: Five smooth stones
17.text: David refused Saul's armor and chose five smooth stones from the brook and his sling — a shepherd's weapons — trusting that the battle was the Lord's.

17.question: Who was the Philistine giant who defied the army of Israel?
What did Goliath propose to the Israelites?
options: A duel: that they send one man to fight him

17.question: Why did Jesse send David to the army's camp?
options: To bring food to his soldier brothers

17.question: For how many days did Goliath defy Israel, morning and evening?
What was promised to whoever defeated Goliath?
options: The king's daughter and great riches

17.question: How did David's eldest brother Eliab react to him in the camp?
options: He was angry and rebuked him

17.question: What experience did David cite to Saul to show he could defeat Goliath?
options: Having killed a lion and a bear protecting the flock

17.question: Why did David refuse Saul's armor?
options: He was not used to it; he chose the sling and five stones

17.question: In whose name did David say he would face Goliath?
options: In the name of the Lord of hosts

17.question: How did David bring down the giant Goliath?
options: With a stone from the sling, striking his forehead

17.question: What did the Philistines do when they saw their champion dead?
options: They fled, and Israel pursued them

17.question: What did Saul ask after David's victory?
options: Whether he wanted to be king

18.title: David and Jonathan
18.text: 'The soul of Jonathan was knit with the soul of David.' Their friendship, sealed by a covenant, is one of the Bible's most famous — loyal even when it cost Jonathan the throne.

18.question: What did Jonathan do when he met David?
options: He made a covenant with him and loved him as himself

18.question: What song of the women made Saul jealous of David?
options: 'Saul hath slain his thousands, and David his ten thousands'

18.question: What did Saul try to do to David while he played the harp?
options: Pin him to the wall with a javelin

18.question: Why did Saul offer his daughter in marriage to David?
options: Hoping the Philistines would kill him

18.question: What unusual price did Saul demand of David for marrying his daughter Michal?
Why did Saul fear David more and more?
options: Because the Lord was with David and he prospered
Because David had a larger army

19.title: David becomes a fugitive
19.text: Saul's envy turns into open persecution. From here on, David — already anointed but still without a throne — lives for years as a fugitive until Saul's death.

19.question: What did Jonathan do when Saul ordered David killed?
options: He interceded for him and reconciled them for a time

19.question: What did Saul try again against David with the javelin?
options: Pin him while he played the harp

19.question: Who helped David escape through a window at night?
How did Michal deceive Saul's messengers seeking David?
options: She put an image in the bed, pretending he was sick
She lied that he had died

19.question: What happened to Saul when he went to Naioth after David?
options: The Spirit of God came on him and he prophesied

20.title: A covenant sealed by oath
20.text: Jonathan loved David 'as his own soul' and made with him an oath of loyalty between their houses — even knowing that David, not he, would be king.

20.question: What did David ask of Jonathan at the start of the chapter?
options: To find out whether Saul truly meant to kill him

20.question: What plan did they agree on to test Saul's intentions?
options: David would miss the new-moon feast

20.question: What covenant did Jonathan make with David?
What sign would Jonathan use to warn David of danger?
options: The direction of the arrows he would shoot

20.question: What would serve as witness of the covenant between David and Jonathan?
What did Saul ask on noticing David's absence at the feast?
options: Why the son of Jesse had not come

20.question: How did Saul react when Jonathan defended David?
options: He raged and threw the javelin at his own son

20.question: How did Jonathan warn David of danger, as agreed?
options: By shooting arrows beyond the boy who fetched them

20.question: How did David and Jonathan part?
options: They wept and renewed their oath of friendship
They went together to the palace

21.title: The holy bread
21.text: The showbread could be eaten only by the priests; Jesus cited this episode of David to teach that mercy is above the letter of the law.

21.question: What did the priest Ahimelech give David at Nob?
options: The holy bread and Goliath's sword

21.question: Who was at Nob and would later inform on the priests to Saul?
What did David do when he found himself in danger before Achish, king of Gath?

22.title: The band of Adullam
22.text: At the cave of Adullam the distressed, the indebted and the discontented gathered to David — about 400 men, the core of his future army.

22.question: Who gathered to David at the cave of Adullam?
options: His family and about 400 men in distress

22.question: Who told Saul that the priest Ahimelech had helped David?
Of what did Saul accuse the priest Ahimelech?
options: Of conspiring with David against him

22.question: What did Saul's guards refuse to do?
options: Kill the priests of the Lord

22.question: Who escaped the massacre at Nob and fled to David?

23.title: Always inquiring of the Lord
23.text: Unlike Saul, David asks the Lord before each step, by means of the ephod brought by Abiathar — and is guided in every decision.

23.question: What did David do for the city of Keilah?
options: He saved it from a Philistine attack

23.question: How did David inquire of the Lord about what to do?
options: By the ephod, with the priest Abiathar

23.question: What did the Lord reveal to David about the men of Keilah?
options: That they would deliver him into Saul's hand
That they would crown him king

23.question: What did Jonathan do when he found David in the wilderness?
options: He strengthened him in God and renewed the covenant

23.question: Who betrayed David's hiding place, telling Saul?
What saved David when Saul had nearly surrounded him in the wilderness?
options: News of a Philistine raid called Saul back

24.title: Respect for the anointed
24.text: Though he could have killed Saul, David did not raise his hand against 'the Lord's anointed' — he chose to wait on God's timing rather than seize the throne by force.

24.question: Where did Saul enter, not knowing David was hiding there?
options: A cave where David was concealed

24.question: What did David do instead of killing Saul in the cave?
options: He cut off only the edge of his robe

24.question: Why did David's heart smite him even for cutting the robe?
How did David prove to Saul he did not want to kill him?
options: He showed him the piece of robe he had cut

24.question: How did Saul react to David's act in the cave?
options: He wept and admitted David was more righteous than he
He crowned him on the spot

25.title: Nabal, the 'fool'
25.text: The name 'Nabal' means 'fool' or 'senseless.' His rudeness nearly causes a massacre — averted by the wisdom of his wife Abigail.

25.question: How are Nabal and his wife Abigail described?
options: Nabal churlish and foolish; Abigail prudent and beautiful

25.question: What did Nabal do when David's men asked for provisions?
options: He invited them to a feast

25.question: What did David prepare to do against Nabal?
options: Attack him with his armed men

25.question: What did Abigail do on learning of the danger?
options: She took food and went to meet David to appease him

25.question: What did Abigail ask of David?
options: That he not shed blood in personal revenge
That he spare only the women

25.question: How did David respond to Abigail's counsel?
options: He praised God and gave up the revenge

25.question: What happened when Abigail told Nabal what she had done?
options: His heart failed and became as a stone

25.question: What happened to Nabal about ten days later?
options: The Lord smote him and he died

25.question: What did David do after Nabal's death?
options: He took Abigail as his wife
He took her goods and left

26.title: Sparing Saul again
26.text: A second time David spares Saul's life, telling Abishai: 'stretch not forth thine hand against the Lord's anointed.' He trusts that God, in due time, will set things right.

26.question: What did David and Abishai do in Saul's camp by night?
options: They went in while everyone slept
They set the tents on fire

26.question: What did David answer when Abishai wanted to kill Saul on the spot?
options: 'Stretch not forth thine hand against the Lord's anointed'

26.question: What did David take from Saul's camp as proof?
How did David show Saul he could have killed him?
options: He called out from afar, showing the spear and water he had taken
He appeared unarmed at the palace

26.question: How did Saul respond this second time?
options: He admitted his sin and blessed David
He tried to kill him again

27.title: Ziklag, David's town
27.text: Achish gave Ziklag to David; the town belonged to the kings of Judah from then on. There David built up his forces, while pretending to be a Philistine ally.

27.question: Where did David go to live, to escape Saul for good?
options: To the land of the Philistines, with Achish of Gath

27.question: What town did Achish give David to dwell in?

28.title: The king who sought what he had banned
28.text: Saul himself had driven the mediums and wizards out of Israel, as the Law commands; but in desperation he turned to a medium at En-dor.

28.question: What had Saul done before, yet now sought in desperation?
options: He had banished mediums, but now sought one

28.question: Why did Saul turn to a medium at En-dor?
options: Because the Lord no longer answered him

28.question: Whom did Saul ask the medium of En-dor to bring up?
What message did 'Samuel' give Saul that night?
options: That the kingdom was given to David and he would die the next day
That he would win the battle
That he would live many years

28.question: What happened to Saul after hearing the prophecy?
options: He fell to the ground, strengthless, and was fed

29.title: Spared an impossible war
29.text: By being sent back, David was spared an impossible situation: having to fight alongside the Philistines against his own people, Israel.

29.question: Why did the Philistine lords not want David in the battle?
options: They feared he would turn against them in the fight

29.question: What did Achish order David before the battle at Gilboa?
options: To go back, so as not to fight against Israel

30.title: 'David encouraged himself in the Lord'
30.text: When the embittered people spoke of stoning him, 'David encouraged himself in the Lord his God' (v6) — a much-cited verse about trusting God in crisis.

30.question: What did David find when he returned to Ziklag?
options: The city burned and the families carried off captive by the Amalekites

30.question: What did David do when the embittered people spoke of stoning him?
options: He encouraged himself in the Lord and inquired of God

30.question: Who helped David find the Amalekites who had raided Ziklag?
options: An Egyptian servant abandoned by the raiders

30.question: How much did David recover from the Amalekites?
options: Everything — nothing was missing, neither people nor goods

30.question: What rule did David set about dividing the spoil?
options: An equal share for those who stayed with the baggage and those who fought

30.question: With whom did David share the spoil, winning their friendship?

31.title: Jabesh-gilead repays the kindness
31.text: The men of Jabesh-gilead recovered the bodies of Saul and his sons — repaying the rescue Saul had given them at the start of his reign (chapter 11).

31.question: What happened to Jonathan and Saul's sons in the battle at mount Gilboa?
options: They were slain by the Philistines

31.question: How did Saul die, so as not to fall alive into Philistine hands?
options: He fell upon his own sword

31.question: Who recovered the bodies of Saul and his sons and buried them with honor?
